findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "create_dispute_resolution_proposal" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Generates EIP-712 typed data for a mutual dispute resolution proposal. Both buyer and seller must sign the same buyerPercentBasisPoints value locally with their…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "sign_receive_with_erc3009_authorization" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

name: expire_escalated_dispute
description: Expires an escalated dispute once the resolver's response period passes without a decision. Permissionless — anyone can call. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: create_dispute_resolution_proposal
description: Generates EIP-712 typed data for a mutual dispute resolution proposal. Both buyer and seller must sign the same buyerPercentBasisPoints value locally with their wallet (EIP-712) before calling resolve_dispute. Returns: typed data structure (domain, types, message).

name: revoke_voucher
description: Revokes a committed (not yet redeemed) voucher on behalf of the seller, returning funds to the buyer. Caller must be the seller assistant. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: approve_exchange_token
description: Grants ERC-20 allowance to the Boson Protocol contract. Must be called before commit_to_offer or deposit_funds when using non-native exchange tokens. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: sign_receive_with_erc3009_authorization
description: Generates EIP-712 typed data for an ERC-3009 ReceiveWithAuthorization (e.g. USDC). Authorizes the spender (default: protocol diamond) to pull `value` from signerAddress between validAfter and validBefore. Sign locally with your wallet (EIP-712); use the resulting signature + nonce to build a Transfe

name: sign_receive_with_erc2612_permit
description: Generates EIP-712 typed data for an EIP-2612 Permit. Authorizes the spender (default: protocol diamond) to pull `value` from signerAddress until `deadline`. Sign locally with your wallet (EIP-712); use the resulting signature to build a TransferAuthorization { strategy: 'EIP2612' } for a meta-tx flo

name: sign_receive_with_permit2
description: Generates EIP-712 typed data for a Uniswap Permit2 PermitTransferFrom. Authorizes the spender (default: protocol diamond) to pull `value` from signerAddress until `deadline`. permit2Address defaults to contracts.permit2 from SDK config; permit2Nonce defaults to a random uint256. Sign locally with yo

name: sign_receive_with_dai_permit
description: Generates EIP-712 typed data for a DAI-style Permit. Authorizes the spender (default: protocol diamond) to pull DAI-style tokens from signerAddress until `expiry`. Sign locally with your wallet (EIP-712); use the result to build a TransferAuthorization { strategy: 'DAIPermit' } for a meta-tx flow. R

name: commit_to_offer
description: Commits to an offer, creating an exchange and minting a voucher NFT. Caller is the buyer (signerAddress) unless 'buyer' param is set. For ERC-20 offers, call approve_exchange_token first. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: commit_to_conditional_offer
description: Commits to a token-gated (conditional) offer using a specific gating tokenId. Caller (signerAddress) must hold the gating token specified by the offer's condition. Use this instead of commit_to_offer for offers created via create_offer_with_condition. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: commit_to_offer_and_redeem_voucher
description: Atomically commits to an offer and immediately redeems the voucher in a single transaction via the OrchestrationHandler. Buyer (signerAddress) must already hold any required ERC-20 allowance (approve_exchange_token). Skips voucher transferability and starts the dispute period clock immediately. Retu

name: commit_to_conditional_offer_and_redeem_voucher
description: Atomically commits to a token-gated (conditional) offer using a specific gating tokenId and immediately redeems the voucher in a single transaction. Buyer must hold the gating token. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: commit_to_buyer_offer
description: Fulfils a buyer-initiated offer (creator='BUYER') by the seller. Caller must be the seller. Provide matching collateral. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: cancel_voucher
description: Cancels a committed voucher. Caller must be the buyer (voucher holder). Buyer forfeits buyerCancellationPenalty; remainder is refunded. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: redeem_voucher
description: Redeems a voucher, signalling physical receipt of goods and starting the dispute period clock. Caller must be the buyer. After redemption, buyer has disputePeriodDurationInMS to raise a dispute. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: complete_exchange
description: Completes an exchange after the dispute period expires, releasing funds to the seller. Can be called by seller after dispute period or by buyer at any time post-redemption. Returns unsigned transaction data.

name: get_sellers_by_address
description: Reads seller entities associated with a specific Ethereum address. Use this to check if signerAddress already has a seller account before calling create_seller. Read-only. Returns: array of seller entities.

name: get_all_products_with_not_voided_variants
description: Reads product groupings with at least one non-voided variant from the Boson subgraph. Useful for storefront displays. Supports pagination and ordering. Read-only.

name: search_products
description: Searches products in the Boson subgraph by keywords matched against title, description, brand, category, and tags. Supports pagination, ordering, filtering, and an includeInvalidOffers flag (default false = only products with currently-valid offers). Read-only. Returns: array of product search resul

name: validate_metadata
description: Validates a metadata object against the Boson Protocol schema (PRODUCT_V1, BUNDLE, BASE, etc.) without storing it. Use before store_*_metadata to catch errors early. Read-only. Returns: validation result with any errors.

name: store_product_v1_metadata
description: Stores a ProductV1 metadata object to IPFS. Call before create_offer to get metadataUri and metadataHash. Returns: { metadataUri: 'ipfs://...', metadataHash: '0x...' }.

name: store_bundle_metadata
description: Stores a Bundle metadata object to IPFS. Prerequisite: call store_bundle_item_product_v1_metadata and/or store_bundle_item_nft_metadata first; include their returned URLs in the items array. Returns: { metadataUri, metadataHash }.

name: store_base_metadata
description: Stores a Base metadata object to IPFS. Use for simple offers that don't require ProductV1 or Bundle structure. Returns: { metadataUri, metadataHash } for use in create_offer.

name: render_contractual_agreement
description: Renders the legal contractual agreement text/HTML for an offer using its offer data and metadata. Use to preview or store the agreement. Returns: rendered agreement string.

name: store_bundle_item_product_v1_metadata
description: Stores a single ProductV1 bundle item's metadata to IPFS. Call once per physical product in a bundle before store_bundle_metadata. Returns: { url: 'ipfs://...' } to include in the items array.

name: store_bundle_item_nft_metadata
description: Stores a single NFT bundle item's metadata to IPFS. Call once per NFT component in a bundle before store_bundle_metadata. Returns: { url: 'ipfs://...' } to include in the items array.

name: register_agent
description: Registers a dACP agent with this MCP server instance, associating its Ethereum identities and roles. The signature proves control of each signerAddress. Server-local state — no configId/signerAddress context needed. Returns: registration confirmation.

name: get_registered_agents
description: Returns all dACP agents registered with this MCP server instance, with their associated protocol entities and roles. No auth required.

name: get_supported_tokens
description: Returns ERC-20 tokens accepted as exchange tokens on the given configId deployment. Use returned addresses in exchangeTokenAddress fields of create_offer. Read-only. Returns: array of { address, name, symbol, decimals }.
